title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish expresses disapproval of Celtics trading Jaylen Brown to the 76ers, likening it to past controversial NBA trades.
Parish, a Celtics legend, believes the trade would have upset Red Auerbach, citing the difficulty of replacing players like Brown.
Despite his criticism, Parish hopes both Brown and the Celtics find mutual respect and honor his contributions in the future.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Celtics' Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown after his trade to the 76ers.
Brown was traded for Paul George and draft picks; he expressed feeling disrespected by the Celtics.
Brown and Tatum shared nine seasons, including a 2024 NBA Finals win, before Brown's trade.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Celtics and Neemias Queta agree on a four-year, $56 million contract extension through the 2030-31 season.
The Celtics picked up Queta's team option for 2026-27, valued at $2,667,944, before agreeing to the extension.
Neemias Queta, originally drafted by the Kings in 2021, averaged 10.2 points and 8.4 rebounds last season with Boston.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates 200 years as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant, established in 1826.
The building, possibly built in 1716, has served as a Revolutionary War newspaper office and a hub for sea trade.
The restaurant serves around 1,500 guests daily with popular items like clam chowder, overseen by chef Americo DiFronzo.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular at Charles River Esplanade features performances and fireworks on July 4.
Gillette Stadium hosts a World Cup quarterfinal match on July 9, impacting Route 1 traffic.
Sail Boston’s Parade of Sail begins on July 11, celebrating with over 60 tall ships in Boston Harbor.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House has hosted six U.S. presidents including Jimmy Carter, Bill Clinton, and Barack Obama.
Celebrities like Matt Damon, Al Pacino, and Meryl Streep have dined at the historic Boston restaurant.
Owner Joseph Milano commissioned a 2003 painting depicting over 50 famous patrons imagined at a party.

title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross of the N.H. State Police died unexpectedly at 41, after 19 years of service.
Ross served in various roles, including commander of the Collision Analysis Unit and was a proud father of three.
A public service is set for July 7 in North Hampton; a fund supports his family after his unexpected death.

title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics showed ‘lack of respect’ after trade to Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown expressed disappointment in the Celtics after his trade to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Brown criticized Celtics executive Brad Stevens for the lack of communication during trade discussions.
The trade involved Brown going to the 76ers in exchange for Paul George and multiple draft picks.

title: Hull man dies after apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 76-year-old Hull resident Robert Nolan died after an apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach on Wednesday evening.
Plymouth County District Attorney's Office reported no foul play is suspected in Nolan's death.
Nolan's body was transported to the Chief Medical Examiner for examination after being pronounced dead at 8:40 p.m.

title: Don Sweeney, Bruins’ retooling plans remain a work in progress
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins traded goalie Joonas Korpisalo to the Rangers for cap relief and acquired defenseman Will Borgen.
Boston signed Connor Clifton and re-signed Jordan Harris, focusing on strengthening their defensive lineup.
Bruins have $5.3 million in cap space and potential roster trades ahead to optimize their lineup.
